2604.18296.title: Exploring Concreteness Through a Figurative Lens
2604.18296.paper_summary: Static concreteness ratings are widely used in NLP, yet a word's concreteness can shift with context, especially in figurative language such as metaphor, where common concrete nouns can take abstract interpretations. While such shifts are evident from context, it remains unclear how LLMs understand concreteness internally. We conduct a layer-wise and geometric analysis of LLM hidden representations across four model families, examining how models distinguish literal vs figurative uses of the same noun and how concreteness is organized in representation space. We find that LLMs separate literal and figurative usage in early layers, and that mid-to-late layers compress concreteness into a one-dimensional direction that is consistent across models. Finally, we show that this geometric structure is practically useful: a single concreteness direction supports efficient figurative-language classification and enables training-free steering of generation toward more literal or more figurative rewrites.
2604.18296.paper_summary_zh: 靜態具體性評分在自然語言處理中被廣泛使用，然而一個詞的具體性可以隨著語境而變化，特別是在隱喻等比喻語言中，常見的具體名詞可以採取抽象的解釋。雖然這種變化在語境中是顯而易見的，但目前仍不清楚大型語言模型（LLMs）如何在內部理解具體性。我們對四個模型家族的LLM隱藏表示進行了逐層和幾何分析，檢查模型如何區分同一名詞的字面與比喻用法，以及具體性在表示空間中的組織方式。我們發現LLMs在早期層次中分離字面和比喻用法，而中到晚期層次則將具體性壓縮為一個在各模型中一致的一維方向。最後，我們展示了這種幾何結構在實際應用中的有效性：單一的具體性方向支持高效的比喻語言分類，並使得在生成過程中無需訓練即可朝著更字面或更比喻的重寫方向進行引導。
2604.18296.author: Saptarshi Ghosh et.al.
